title: Tain Bo Cuailnge: The Cattle Raid of Cooley
source: Book of the Dun Cow (Lebor na hUidre)
description: The Irish Iliad: Cuchulainn alone defends Ulster against the armies of Connacht.
introduction: The Tain Bo Cuailnge is the central epic of Irish mythology — the story of Queen Medb's invasion of Ulster to seize the great Brown Bull, and the teenage hero Cuchulainn's single-handed defense of the province while its warriors lie stricken by a curse. Preserved in manuscripts from the 12th century but composed centuries earlier, it is the longest and most important text in the Ulster Cycle.

translation.original_text: Feacht n-óen do Ailill ocus do Meidb iar ndérgud a rígleptha dóib i Cruachain Aí.
translation.text: Once upon a time, when Ailill and Medb had spread their royal bed in Cruachan Ai.

translation.original_text: Is and asbert Cú Chulaind: 'Ní géb-sa bás co n-aprainn gním n-erred.'
translation.text: Then Cuchulainn said: 'I will not die until I have performed a warrior's deed.'
